channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Asian-Canadians and Jewish Community Forge Solidarity Against Antisemitism
item.description: After a Montreal synagogue was firebombed in June 2026, a coalition of Asian communities—including Cambodian, Chinese, Vietnamese, and Filipino groups—united to denounce antisemitism and support the J...

item.title: Jimmy Alapag Returns to Philippine Basketball After NBA Stint
item.description: Jimmy Alapag, a former NBA player, has decided to return to the Philippines after his stint in the United States. The article details his career trajectory, including his time in the NBA and his decis...

item.title: Nigeria Extends Condolences to Qatar Following Former Emir Sheikh Hamad's Passing
item.description: The Federal Government of Nigeria has expressed deep sorrow over the death of Sheikh Hamad bin Khalifa Al Thani, the former Emir of Qatar, who passed away on July 12, 2026. In a statement by the Minis...

item.title: Yosemite Ventures Expands Biotech Portfolio with Focus on Epigenetic Editing and Non-Invasive Cancer Therapies
item.description: The article discusses Yosemite Ventures' strategic investments in biotechnology, highlighting two key companies: Tune Therapeutics and Histosonics. Tune Therapeutics is developing epigenetic editing t...

item.title: Philippines Expands Mental Health Support in Schools with New Counselor Associates
item.description: The Philippines faces a severe shortage of licensed guidance counselors, with only around 4,000 available as of 2022. This gap has raised concerns about students' mental health and safety, especially ...

item.title: NDLEA Arrests Woman With 5.75kg Heroin Hidden In Luggage At Abuja Airport
item.description: The National Drug Law Enforcement Agency (NDLEA) arrested a 38-year-old South African woman at Abuja's Nnamdi Azikiwe International Airport after discovering 5.75kg of heroin concealed in her luggage....

item.title: EPRA Announces July Electricity Charges, Raising Power Bills for Kenyans
item.description: The Energy and Petroleum Regulatory Authority (EPRA) has announced new electricity charges for July 2026, which will significantly increase power bills for Kenyans. The adjustments come amid rising fu...

item.title: Police search for Soweto homeowner after suspected human organs and hijacked health department vehicle are found
item.description: Police are searching for the owner of a property in Naledi, Soweto, after officers discovered two buckets containing suspected human organs and a hijacked National Department of Health vehicle that wa...

item.title: Zelensky Holds Officials Accountable for Weapon Storage in Civilian Area Amid Escalating Conflict
item.description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky has condemned the placement of weapons warehouses in a civilian area following a Russian strike that killed 10 people in Vyshneve, a town on the outskirts of Kyi...

item.title: Argentina advances to World Cup semifinals after dramatic extra-time win over Switzerland
item.description: Argentina's Lionel Messi-led team secured a thrilling 3-1 victory over 10-man Switzerland in the FIFA World Cup quarterfinals, advancing to the semifinals. The match, held in Kansas City, saw Julian A...

item.title: JAB Expands to Regions to Improve Justice Access as Editors Push for Education Reforms
item.description: The Judges and Magistrates Association of Tanzania (JAB) is relocating its members to regional areas to decentralize judicial services, aiming to improve access to justice in underserved regions. This...

item.title: Argentina advances to World Cup semi-finals after defeating Switzerland in thrilling quarter-final clash
item.description: In a dramatic World Cup quarter-final clash, Argentina edged out Switzerland 2-1 in extra time, securing their place in the semi-finals against England. The match, held at the Al Bayt Stadium in Qatar...

item.title: Taylor Swift Pays Over $160,000 for NYC Permit to Host MSG Wedding
item.description: New York City mayor Zohran Mamdani has confirmed that Taylor Swift and her husband Travis Kelce paid over $160,000 (£119,000) to secure a permit for their high-profile wedding at Madison Square Garden...

item.title: Rock City Festival 2026 Boosts Arts, Sports, and Tourism in Lake Zone
item.description: The Rock City 2026 festival, set to run from August to December in Mwanza, aims to showcase youth talent and boost economic development in Tanzania’s Lake Zone. Organized by Feyfay Investment, the eve...

item.title: Lorry Collision Kills Two at Kisii Petrol Station
item.description: A tragic incident occurred on Sunday morning when a lorry lost control and crashed into eight parked trucks at Tosha Petrol Station along Kisii-Keroka Road. The collision, which happened around 4:30am...

item.title: NEMC takes legal action against 27 businesses for environmental violations
item.description: The National Environment Management Council (NEMC) has initiated legal proceedings against 27 businesses and mining operations in the Eastern Lake Victoria Zone for breaching environmental regulations...

item.title: President Ramaphosa Honors 110th Anniversary of Delville Wood Battle
item.description: President Cyril Ramaphosa is set to attend the 110th commemoration of the Battle of Delville Wood at the South African Memorial in Longueval, France. This event marks a significant historical mileston...

item.title: Venomous Snakes Escape into City Amid Floods Caused by Typhoon Maysak
item.description: Residents in Hengzhou, southern China, are facing a terrifying situation after around 900 venomous snakes escaped into nearby communities following devastating floods caused by Typhoon Maysak. The sna...

item.title: Peller & Jarvis Announce August 1 Wedding, Unveil ₦1 Million Aso Ebi Package
item.description: Peller and Jarvis have officially announced their white wedding date as August 1, 2026, with the ceremony expected to be one of the most-watched influencer weddings in Nigeria this year. The couple re...

item.title: Lindsey Graham Dies from Sudden Illness; Trump Reacts with Tributes
item.description: U.S. Senator Lindsey Graham died on Saturday night at age 71 from a 'brief and sudden illness,' according to his office. Emergency responders were called to his Capitol Hill home after reports of card...
